spec_match.rationale: The selectors and property bindings are correct and accurately demonstrate standard text-decoration-skip-spaces modes.

interactive.rationale: Provides responsive button selectors that update the prose article's links in real-time alongside detailed descriptive text updates.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the parent description perfectly: provides a realistic body-text prose demo with zoomed comparisons showing large-scale differences.

edge_cases.rationale: Provides realistic prose paragraphs with multiple adjacent multi-word links, illustrating why inline spaces confuse visual structure.

references.rationale: Includes direct links to the ChromeStatus entry, W3C draft spec, and companion concept pages.

design_system.rationale: Excellent compliance with fonts, variable-based colors, layout blocks, and spacing conventions.

openQuestions.title: Add an interactive magnifier or high-contrast zoom window
openQuestions.detail: The zoom-row currently displays the comparative blocks at a slightly larger 1.35rem font size. We could make this even better by incorporating a high-magnification lens or high-contrast zoom window that zooms in at 3x scale on the character edges (e.g., between 'and' and the next word) to let authors clearly inspect the anti-aliasing and visual gap left by space-skipping.

summary: An excellent, fully compliant, and highly educational concept demo. It places CSS4 space-skipping within the context of realistic body prose, demonstrating the practical problem it solves. It has robust support detection, is fully interactive, and matches all repository criteria.
